tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, mild ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), with low specific gravity. These findings suggest possible renal dysfunction, likely of tubular origin, and possible underlying metabolic or hepatic involvement. Further nephrological and clinical evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
